notes: SHIP v1.14.227 — FORGE 3D STAGE 2: THE HERO (per HANDOFF-forge-hero-scene §3+§4; Stage-1 aisle DEVICE-VERIFIED by owner). The F-04 slot becomes a data-driven hero rack + hero-only spotlight rig, built ON the verified Stage-1 aisle. The hero IS a Forge rack — same buildSingleRack chassis/face/bezel/LED-bar/glow/ports at the same 1.8x3.8 scale — differing ONLY in: (a) interior driven by real Master data, (b) a brighter accent label (#5cf2ff) with the real rack ID, (c) the spotlight. WHAT: (1) NEW top-level forge3d_resolveHeroRack() reuses the app's CANONICAL resolvers (One door per feature): activeContext_get().rack -> activeContext_get().racks[0] -> nowtab_resolveDep()+deploy_loadRacks()[0] -> Master-first mscope_master()+master_rackToElevation(); returns {rack,deployment,source} or null. NULL -> the generic Stage-1 hero (valid Zero-state per Design Law 2 — NO toast). (2) buildHeroRack() inner fn: same Forge chassis/face/bezel/LED/glow/ports, but the interior renders one visible row per Master device (rack.slots[] {uStart,uEnd,type,name,model,serial,dns}), top-anchored, up to 10 visible (§3.1 'keep it simple'); each row = Forge divider + status dot + activity LED + a full-width TRANSPARENT hit-box (userData.dev) so the whole row is gloved-tappable. Label via makeRackLabel(realRackId, '#5cf2ff'). (3) buildRacks branches n===4 -> buildHeroRack when _heroRack resolved, else generic buildSingleRack. (4) buildHeroSpotlight() (§4): SpotLight(0xeaf6ff,3.4,6,PI/5,0.5,1.5) above-front aimed at the hero + cyan front-left PointLight + violet back-right PointLight + rim DirectionalLight from behind + a cyan floor pool; short ranges centered on x=1.25,z=0 so they die before F-03/F-05; added ON TOP of the dim §2.7 baseline; only built when _heroRack exists. (5) TAP: raycast on a non-drag pointer-up (moved-threshold 6px distinguishes tap from orbit) hits ONLY heroMeshes -> window._rmConnHit={id:rackId} + openRmDevice(dev) (the .218 render3D pattern, verbatim); non-hero racks are inert (not in heroMeshes). (6) teardown clears heroMeshes; the spotlight lights live in the scene and are freed by the existing traverse-dispose + forceContextLoss. STATUS DEFERRED (correct — Part A not shipped): a grep confirmed NO per-node status source (phantom_node_status_v1 absent); every hero device defaults to racked/green (0x00ee55); the status seam is wired (comment marks where pending 0xf0a63c / fault 0xff5f67 + fault-pulse plug in when Part A's serial-keyed status lands). Uses only r128 APIs (SpotLight, Raycaster, Vector2, PointLight/Directional, MeshBasicMaterial — all present). DEVIATION (reported): interior is the §3.1 SIMPLE 'one row per device, top-anchored' option (not real-U-scaled) + type is metadata-for-the-tap (the Forge hero shows STATUS-colored dots per §0, not type-colored trays — that's the .218 render3D convention, a different surface). ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (3 lenses, per-finding verified): lifecycle/single-context lens CLEAN; TWO findings CONFIRMED + FIXED before push — (HIGH) the Master-first resolver picked Object.keys(racksByCab)[0], but racksByCab is cable-parsed-first so [0] is usually a HOST-LESS cable-endpoint cab → master_rackToElevation returns slots:[] and the truthy-[] guard shipped a spotlit-but-EMPTY hero on the common Master-only path (host-less-cab class); FIXED by scanning racksByCab for the first cab with slots.length>0, else falling to the generic hero. (LOW) tstart handled 1- and 2-touch but not 3+, leaving moved stale-false so a 3-finger rest-and-lift could fire the hero raycast; FIXED with an else branch (3+ touches → moved=true, never a tap). Gates: node --check sw.js + 4 inline blocks (module pre-checked in isolation) · CSS braces (no CSS change) · CRLF · three-stamp lockstep .226->.227 · resolver + buildHeroRack + buildHeroSpotlight + raycast present · single-context guards intact. ROLLBACK: additive to forge3d_render + 1 new resolver fn; git revert clean. DEVICE-VERIFY (batch, HARD STOP): update to v1.14.227, SYS->FORGE 3D (with a Master/deployment loaded): (1) the F-04 slot is now the HERO — same rack construction as its neighbors (NOT a different-looking rack), brighter cyan label = the real rack ID; (2) its interior rows reflect the real device count (not 10 identical), dots green (racked default); (3) the SPOTLIGHT lights the hero, aisle stays dark, neighbors visible only by their own LEDs — confirm NO spill onto F-03/F-05; (4) tap a hero row -> the device sheet opens for that device; (5) orbit-drag does NOT open a sheet (only a clean tap does); (6) tapping a NON-hero rack does nothing; (7) with NO Master loaded -> generic Stage-1 aisle (no hero, no error); (8) toggle FLAT|3D rack view then FORGE 3D and back -> no orphan GPU (single-context guard); (9) ?legacy=1 untouched. Do NOT proceed to Stage 3 (dimmer) until the hero verifies. STILL OWED (batch): .220/.221/.224/.225/.226 (+.227). (.222 Forge aisle + .223 deploy card device-verified.) NEXT: Stage 3 (background dimmer §5), then Stage 4 (features from MOCKUP-HYBRID-v14.html: zone map/manifest/tour/explode/rack-switching; r134->r128 + FontAwesome port), then Part A (per-node status → real hero status colors).
